content:

Systems can generate hypotheses, interpret data and suggest ways to develop medicines.

title: Why AI cannot do good science without humans
content:
Nature, Published online: 19 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01551-3

With the arrival of ‘AI scientists’, it’s as well to remember that human wisdom, empathy and sheer messiness are as much part of progress as are process and efficiency.

title: Could this synthetic egg bring back extinct birds? Researchers urge caution
content:
Nature, Published online: 19 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01535-3

Colossal Biosciences says its artificial egg has de-extinction and conservation potential.

title: The uncritical adoption of AI in science is alarming — we urgently need guard rails
content:
Nature, Published online: 19 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01557-x

Artificial intelligence is rapidly accelerating scientific output, but risks narrowing inquiry, weakening judgement and undermining how scientists are trained.

title: Researchers who use hallucinated references to face arXiv ban
content:
Nature, Published online: 19 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01595-5

The preprint server is the latest to impose stiff penalties on authors who contribute to AI ‘slop’ — but not everyone is convinced it’s the right approach.

title: DNA-folding changes block production of self-directed antibodies
content:
Nature, Published online: 19 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01329-7

DNA rearrangements in immune-system B cells generate diverse antibody-encoding genes and help to avoid producing antibodies that target the body’s own tissues.

title: ‘It is incredible’: How AI is transforming mathematics
content:
Nature, Published online: 19 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01553-1

Thanks to some surprising advances, mathematicians are starting to realize that artificial intelligence could radically alter their profession.

title: Flexible freestanding Ag2Se thermoelectric films with high performance for sap flow monitoring
content: Ultralow-content CNT (0.9 wt %) enables freestanding flexibility in Ag1.8Se while enhancing thermoelectric performance via energy filtering. The Ag1.8Se/CNT films exhibit a high room-temperature power factor and small bending radius. The ∼10-μm-thick freestanding films enable flexible devices with substantial power output. This strategy provides a sustainable route to high-performance inorganic thermoelectrics.

title: Suppressed calendar aging losses via dynamic interfaces in metal anodes
content: While metal anodes offer high energy density, their practical use is limited by severe “calendar aging” during rest. This study investigates how the formation of a dynamic, reversible interface on Mg can kinetically suppress calendar aging, providing a new design principle for achieving the long-term shelf life required for reactive metal anodes.

title: A twist away from hydrogen
content:
Nature Energy, Published online: 19 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02066-x

Electron spins are often not considered in electrocatalysis, yet they can play a pivotal role in determining reaction pathways. New research suggests that chiral (twisted) copper can generate spin-polarized charge carriers during CO2 electroreduction and suppress undesired hydrogen evolution.

title: Securing US electric vehicle battery supply requires domestic action and international sourcing
content:
Nature Energy, Published online: 19 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02045-2

Expanding domestic production and implementing demand-side strategies can substantially narrow material shortages for US electric vehicle batteries, but these efforts still fall short of meeting projected 2035 demand. To assure US battery supplies, the USA must support investments across all domestic supply chain stages, reduce and shift battery material demand, and continue to secure international supplies.

title: Improving the stability of monolithic perovskite/silicon tandems against reverse-bias stress using graded dielectric layers
content:
Nature Energy, Published online: 19 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02067-w

Perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells degrade under reverse bias. Wang et al. introduce graded dielectric layers that smooth the electric field and reduce tunnelling and interfacial reactions, improving reverse-bias tolerance.

title: Evaluating strategies to address material supply–demand gaps in the US electric vehicle battery supply chain
content:
Nature Energy, Published online: 19 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02046-1

As the USA works to secure supplies of electric vehicle battery materials, Lu et al. show that this will require investment across the domestic supply chain, demand-side strategies and continued international sourcing.

title: Anion-reduction catalytic centres regulate interfacial solvation structures for fast-charging Si anodes
content:
Nature Energy, Published online: 19 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02074-x

Interfacial solvation structures are key to stable fast charging in Si anodes. Qiao and colleagues show that S-vacancy-driven anion-reduction catalysis regulates these structures to form a LiF-rich solid electrolyte interphase, enabling ultrafast charging and long cycle life.

title: Close-space sublimation as a versatile deposition process for efficient perovskite silicon tandem solar cells
content:
Nature Energy, Published online: 19 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02068-9

Research on perovskite solar cells shows that scaling up production will require faster, more reliable film-making methods. This work introduces a simple high-rate process that forms high-quality wide-bandgap films for efficient single-junction and tandem devices.

title: Publisher Correction: Theory-driven design of high-valence metal sites for water oxidation confirmed using in situ soft X-ray absorption
content:
Nature Chemistry, Published online: 19 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41557-026-02170-1

Publisher Correction: Theory-driven design of high-valence metal sites for water oxidation confirmed using in situ soft X-ray absorption
